title: Quiz: Quality Control Applications
description: Test your understanding of computational QC applications

questions.question: Which computational method is most appropriate for predicting UV-Vis spectra?
questions.explanation: TDDFT calculates excited state energies and transition properties, providing excitation wavelengths (λmax) and oscillator strengths (intensities) needed for UV-Vis prediction with typical accuracy of ±10-20 nm.

questions.question: What scale factor is typically applied to DFT-calculated vibrational frequencies?
questions.explanation: DFT typically overestimates vibrational frequencies due to the harmonic approximation and other factors. Scale factors of 0.96-0.98 (depending on functional and basis set) bring calculated frequencies into better agreement with experiment.

questions.question: What does ICH M7 guidance address?
questions.explanation: ICH M7 provides guidance for assessing and controlling DNA reactive (mutagenic) impurities in pharmaceuticals. Computational methods play a key role in structure-based assessment of mutagenic potential.

questions.question: In predicting oxidative degradation, which molecular property is most informative?
questions.explanation: The HOMO (Highest Occupied Molecular Orbital) indicates where electrons are most easily removed. Sites with high HOMO density are most susceptible to oxidation, making HOMO localization the key predictor for oxidative degradation sites.

questions.question: Computational spectral predictions can replace experimental spectra for regulatory submissions.
questions.explanation: False. Computational predictions are supportive evidence but cannot replace experimental data for regulatory submissions. Regulators require actual measured spectra for identity testing and impurity characterization. Computation guides and supports experimental work.

questions.question: What information should be documented for GMP-relevant computational predictions? (Select all)
questions.explanation: For GMP-quality documentation, you must record the software/version, input structures, parameters, results, and comparison to experimental data. This enables reproducibility and regulatory review. Limitations should also be stated.
